competitor_patterns: Bikerumor leads with a 24,000+ word mega-guide updated weekly — Google rewards freshness signals ('updated May 21, 2026') and depth; their H2s are pure product names (Julbo, Oakley, Tifosi) which capture branded + category queries simultaneously

Top-ranking pages universally include FAQ sections (Bikerumor, Cyclist) and comparison tables — these generate featured snippets and People Also Ask placements that multiply impression share without extra pages

Competitor titles use the pattern '[Best/Top] Cycling [Sunglasses/Glasses] [Year] [Tested/Reviewed]' — the year token + credibility word ('Tested', 'Reviewed') is present in every top-3 result, signalling editorial authority to both Google and users

Demonsun ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a dedicated category/landing page, not a blog post — transactional keyword intent is served by a product-focused page, not editorial content

Cyclist.co.uk includes a 'How do we test and why trust us?' H2 — explicit trust/authority signals embedded in content structure help E-E-A-T scoring, which is critical for YMYL-adjacent product recommendation pages
our_gaps: No comparison tables in any published posts — all three recent posts lack tables, while Bikerumor and 220triathlon use them as a core structural element; tables drive featured snippets and reduce bounce for comparison-intent visitors

Word counts are 2,700–3,400 words vs Bikerumor's 24,361 — even Cyclist at 6,600 words outpaces us significantly; for 'best cycling glasses' head terms, thin content cannot compete without compensating with extreme specificity or unique data

No dedicated landing page for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' — Demonsun owns this transactional keyword with a product page; our blog posts discuss the feature but there is no conversion-optimised, keyword-targeted page for this high-intent query

Zero explicit E-E-A-T signals in published content — no author bio with cycling credentials, no test methodology section, no 'how we tested' language; top competitors make trust-building a structural element, not an afterthought

Existing posts target competitive head terms (road cycling sunglasses, anti-fog cycling sunglasses) without owning a unique data angle — competitors publish lab test data, ride-hour counts, and weight measurements; Velluto's 25g USP is mentioned but never made the centrepiece of a dedicated spec-comparison piece
keyword_opportunities: lightweight cycling sunglasses under 30g — highly specific, matches Velluto's 25g frame USP exactly, low competition from major publishers, transactional intent
cycling glasses with interchangeable lenses road bike — long-tail version of Demonsun's ranking keyword; adding 'road bike' filters out MTB competition and matches StradaPro's positioning

best cycling glasses for foggy mornings — problem-specific query with clear anti-fog intent; 220triathlon and Bikerumor don't have dedicated pages for this; aligns directly with StradaPro's built-in anti-fog system

cycling sunglasses 30 day trial — near-zero competition for this exact phrase; Velluto's risk-free trial is a unique commercial differentiator that no competitor can replicate; captures bottom-of-funnel hesitant buyers

Italian cycling sunglasses design — low-volume but low-competition cultural/lifestyle angle; targets style-conscious road cyclists who cross-shop with fashion eyewear; no major cycling media outlet owns this query
next_post_guidelines: Target 'lightweight cycling sunglasses' as the primary keyword for the next post — build the entire piece around weight as a performance variable; use a comparison table with frame weights (25g Velluto vs 28g Oakley Sutro Lite vs 26g POC Devour vs 30g+ average) to own the featured snippet slot for weight-comparison queries

Add a mandatory comparison table to every post going forward — minimum 5 products, columns: Weight, Lens System, Anti-Fog, UV Rating, Price, Trial Policy; this single structural element closes the biggest gap vs Bikerumor and 220triathlon simultaneously

Include a 'How We Tested' or 'Our Testing Criteria' section in every post (minimum 150 words) — describe real ride conditions (distance, weather, intensity), list specific metrics evaluated; this directly addresses the E-E-A-T gap vs Cyclist and Bikerumor

Write titles using the proven competitor pattern: '[Adjective] Cycling [Product] [Year]: [Specific Promise]' — example: 'Lightweight Cycling Sunglasses 2026: The 25g Benchmark Tested'; include the year and a concrete spec or outcome in every title

For every post mentioning interchangeable lenses, include a dedicated H2 specifically named 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses: What to Look For' — this H2 text matches the exact query Demonsun ranks #1 for and can capture clicks from within a broader post without a separate page

Embed the 30-day trial CTA as a named section, not just inline text — use an H3 like 'Test It on Real Rides: 30-Day Risk-Free Trial' so it appears in page outlines and can be indexed as a trust signal; this is a unique conversion lever no competitor can copy

Increase target word count to minimum 4,500 words for any post targeting head terms ('best cycling glasses', 'road cycling sunglasses') — use the additional words for: extended FAQ (8+ questions), a detailed buying guide section, and a 'common mistakes' subsection that captures problem-aware search queries
seo_quick_wins: Add a structured comparison table (HTML table or shortcode) to every post — use schema markup (ItemList or Product schema) so Google can render it as a rich result; this single change addresses both the snippet gap and the E-E-A-T gap simultaneously across all existing and future posts

Add FAQ schema (FAQPage structured data) to every post that already has a FAQ section — all three recent posts have FAQ sections but structured data implementation status is unknown; confirmed FAQ schema generates People Also Ask appearances which can double impression share for zero additional content work

Standardise meta title format across all posts to: '[Primary Keyword] [Year]: [Unique Hook] | Velluto' (max 60 chars) — current titles are too long and bury the year token; example fix: 'Lightweight Cycling Glasses 2026: 25g Tested | Velluto' — the year signals freshness, the spec signals credibility, the brand anchors the SERP result
meta_title_fixes: No GSC data available — meta title fix recommendations cannot be generated without click and impression data per query. Once GSC is connected and 28 days of data is collected, low-CTR queries (CTR below 2% with >100 impressions) should be prioritised for title rewrites using the format: [Primary Keyword] [Year]: [Specific Differentiator] | Velluto
content_quick_wins: No GSC data available — near-top-3 keyword identification requires position data. Once GSC is active, any keyword ranking positions 4–10 with >50 impressions/month should be targeted with: (1) adding a comparison table, (2) expanding the relevant section by 300+ words with specific specs, (3) adding internal links from the two most recent posts to reinforce topical authority on that query
geo_angles: Structure every product-feature post with a direct-answer opening paragraph (40–60 words) that names the product, the specific claim, and the proof — e.g. 'The Velluto StradaPro weighs 25g, making it the lightest cycling sunglasses in its price tier. It uses a tool-free click-in lens system with two included lenses (clear + high-contrast) and a 30-day ride trial.' This format matches the extraction pattern used by Perplexity and Google AI Overviews for product recommendation queries

Publish a dedicated 'Cycling Sunglasses Comparison 2026' page with a structured data-rich table comparing Velluto StradaPro against Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour, Tifosi Rail, and Julbo Density across 8 objective specs (weight, lens system, UV rating, anti-fog, trial policy, price) — AI answer engines preferentially cite structured comparison sources when users ask 'what is the best lightweight cycling sunglasses' type queries, and this page becomes a citable authority source

Add a 'Key Facts' summary box at the top of every post (weight, lens count, UV rating, anti-fog: yes/no, trial: 30 days, price) using consistent HTML markup — Perplexity and ChatGPT browsing extract structured factual snippets from near-the-top page content; a consistent summary box trains these systems to associate Velluto with specific verifiable claims, increasing citation probability in AI-generated answers about cycling eyewear
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Oakley — angle: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro: Is 3g and €80 Worth It?' | Headline idea: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs StradaPro: The Weight and Price Test (2026)' | Key differentiator: frame weight (25g vs ~28g) + price gap + 30-day trial Oakley doesn't offer | Velluto advantage to lead with: 'You pay a premium for the Oakley logo — StradaPro is lighter, comes with two lenses, and lets you test it for 30 days on real rides'

COMPETITOR: Bikerumor — angle: 'Every Glass Bikerumor Recommends vs Our One Pick: Why We Chose Depth Over Breadth' | Headline idea: 'Bikerumor Best Cycling Glasses 2026: What the List Misses About Lightweight Italian Design' | Key differentiator: Bikerumor tests 18 glasses and picks winners by category; Velluto wins the specific 'ultralight + interchangeable + anti-fog + value' combination that their format can't highlight | Velluto advantage to lead with: 'Roundups optimise for variety — this post optimises for the rider who wants one glass that does everything under 30g'

COMPETITOR: Demonsun — angle: 'Demon vs Velluto Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses: Italian Design vs Bulgarian Sport' | Headline idea: 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses 2026: Demon Sunglasses vs Velluto StradaPro Compared' | Key differentiator: Demonsun ranks #1 for the exact query 'cycling glasses with interchangeable lenses' — a direct comparison post captures their branded traffic and converts comparison-intent visitors | Velluto advantage to lead with: '30-day trial, anti-fog system, and 25g frame weight — three things the Demon catalogue doesn't offer as a combined package'
